nativeRange.habitat: Sheltered, shallow tropical reefs from estuaries to outer reef lagoons, typically 2-12 m depth; hides among live or dead coral branches.
nativeRange.biotope: Coral-rubble reefs with abundant algae growth on hard substrate.

decorPreferences: abundant live rock with microalgae growth
caves and crevices in rockwork for perching
tightly fitted lid to prevent jumping

diet.notes: Obligate microalgae grazer that browses film and filamentous algae from live rock and glass. Generally advised to supplement with dried nori, spirulina sheets, and algae-based frozen or prepared foods, especially in tanks without abundant natural algae growth. Has been known to slowly starve in tanks scrubbed clean of algae.

avoidWith: other Atrosalarias or similar combtooth blennies
small-polyp stony corals it may nip

summary: A peaceful algae-grazing combtooth blenny from sheltered Indo-Pacific and Red Sea reefs, prized for its uniformly dark coloration and appetite for film and filamentous algae. Generally reef-safe with caveats and a known jumper.
careNotes: Best housed in a mature tank with abundant live rock that has had time to develop microalgae and filamentous algae growth; tanks kept aggressively clean tend to under-feed this species. Generally peaceful toward other fish but has been reported to be territorial toward conspecifics and other Atrosalarias-type blennies — if keeping more than one, simultaneous acclimation as a pair is generally advised. Has been known to nip small-polyp stony corals and tridacnid clam mantles in some systems, so it is commonly considered reef-safe with caution rather than universally reef-safe. Like most blennies, has been reported to be a strong jumper and a tightly fitted lid is generally advised. Source spread on adult size: BlueZoo Aquatics 4 in (~10 cm), fishy.wiki 11.4 cm, FishBase 14.5 cm TL — recorded as 10-14 cm. Source spread on minimum tank size: BlueZoo 24 US gal (~90 L), LiveAquaria 30 US gal (~115 L), fishy.wiki 113 L — biased upward to 150 L to give this active grazer adequate rock surface area for foraging and swimming room. LiveAquaria and BlueZoo retailer pages did not specify lifespan; 2-6 years is an aquarium-hobby consensus estimate for similar combtooth blennies and should be treated as approximate.
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing; eggs are demersal and adhesive, anchored to substrate by filamentous pads, with planktonic larvae found in shallow coastal waters. Has rarely been bred in home aquariums; rearing the planktonic larvae through metamorphosis is generally considered difficult and not routinely accomplished.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry reference: family Blenniidae, native to reefs of Java, min 30 gal, peaceful with similar tankmates but territorial toward conspecifics, reef-compatible with caveat (may nip SPS polyps and clam mantles), obligate microalgae diet, strong jumper needing tight lid. Adult size and lifespan not stated.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Cross-check: adult size 4 in, min tank 24 gal, intermediate care, peaceful, herbivore, reef-compatible. Also lists alternate trade names (Dusky Blenny, Brown Coral Blenny, Black Blenny, Brown Combtooth Blenny).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Taxonomy and wild biology cross-check: Family Blenniidae, Order Blenniiformes, max 14.5 cm TL, Indo-Pacific incl. Red Sea, depth 2-12 m, preferred temp range 24.9-29.3 C (mean 27.2 C), oviparous with distinct pairing, demersal adhesive eggs, planktonic larvae.

additional.name: fishy.wiki
additional.notes: Third independent confirmation of water parameters: 24-27 C, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, dKH 8-12, min 113 L, adult 11.4 cm, moderate care, peaceful but conspecific-territorial, obligate microalgae diet.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing; demersal adhesive eggs and planktonic larvae rarely reared in home aquariums.
